summary:
The rectum shows highest radiosensitivity among these organs when exposed to pelvic external beam radiation because it has a thinner wall than others such as the bladder or ovaries. This makes option \(\textbf{(D)}\)Rectum the best choice for this scenario involving treatment of uterine cancer via pelvic irradiation. Agent2 reasoned that based on known radiobiological properties, the organ at greatest risk from radiotherapy within the specified area would be the rectum. 

The summary focuses only on essential information regarding which specific organ was identified by analysis of tissue response characteristics post-irradiation exposure pertinent directly towards answering about relative sensitivities under described conditions without extraneous details not critical to core understanding required here.
